principle.name: Systematic Analysis
principle.description: Perform comprehensive analysis by following a structured approach for each file, covering all required dimensions (bugs, performance, naming, ESLint, code smells, security)
principle.rationale: Ensures thorough coverage and prevents missing critical issues

example.good: Follow the complete checklist: bugs → performance → naming → ESLint → code smells → security
example.bad: Skipping steps or only focusing on one dimension

principle.name: Severity Classification
principle.description: Classify all findings by severity level (Critical, High, Medium, Low) to help prioritize fixes
principle.rationale: Enables developers to focus on the most important issues first

example.good: Classify as Critical: "Null pointer exception at line 42 - application will crash"

principle.name: Non-Invasive Approach
principle.description: Analyze code without making commits or modifications. All findings should be documented for manual review and approval
principle.rationale: Preserves repository integrity and gives developers control over changes

example.good: Document: "Line 15: Consider using const instead of let (ESLint: prefer-const)"

principle.name: Progressive Analysis
principle.description: Process directories systematically, tracking progress to allow resumption if interrupted
principle.rationale: Enables handling large repositories and prevents re-analysis

example.good: Use .analysis-progress file to track completed directories
example.bad: Starting over from scratch if interrupted

principle.name: Context-Aware Analysis
principle.description: Consider the file type, language, and project context when analyzing code
principle.rationale: Different file types require different analysis approaches and rules

example.good: Apply TypeScript-specific rules to .ts files, React-specific patterns to .tsx files
example.bad: Using the same analysis approach for all file types

convention.rule: Variable names should be descriptive and follow project conventions (camelCase for JavaScript/TypeScript)
Function names should be verbs or verb phrases describing what they do
Class names should be nouns or noun phrases using PascalCase
Functions should be focused and do one thing
Use early returns to reduce nesting

pitfall.why_problematic: Can cause runtime errors and crashes
pitfall.correct_approach: Always check for null/undefined before accessing properties or calling methods

pitfall.description: Using any type instead of specific types
pitfall.why_problematic: Defeats TypeScript's type safety and can hide bugs
pitfall.correct_approach: Use specific types or create proper interfaces/types

pitfall.why_problematic: Errors can go unhandled, causing unexpected behavior
pitfall.correct_approach: Use try-catch blocks and proper error handling patterns

pitfall.description: Memory leaks from event listeners or subscriptions
pitfall.why_problematic: Can cause performance degradation and crashes over time
pitfall.correct_approach: Clean up event listeners and subscriptions in useEffect cleanup or component unmount
